title: 7 Signs a Man of God Is Becoming a Leader Worth Following
caption:
Leadership isn't a title. It's a pattern of behavior over time.

He apologizes first, even when it costs him the high ground. He serves when it doesn't help his reputation. He says the hard thing to someone's face, gently — not behind their back. He shows up when it costs him something: time, sleep, money, comfort.

He takes responsibility for outcomes, not just intentions. "I meant well" is not leadership. He prays for the men he's leading — by name, in private, asking God to do what he can't do alone.

And the clearest sign: he's more teachable now than he was three years ago. The man getting harder to correct isn't growing. The man getting easier to reach is.

This is what Christ-shaped leadership looks like. Not authority over people. Willingness to go first into the cost.

Save this for the man who wants to lead but isn't sure what it actually looks like.

#faith #leadership #christianmen #godlymen #manofgod #thevision #christled #christianity #servantleadership
theme: He leads by going first into the cost.
